feed.title: Against Allowing Quantum Recovery of Bitcoin

entry.title: Against Allowing Quantum Recovery of Bitcoin
entry.summary:
The ongoing discussions surrounding Bitcoin and the potential implementation of a post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) scheme highlight significant concerns and considerations within the development community. The primary consensus is rooted in the necessity of a "freeze fork" should quantum computing (QC) become an undeniable reality or appear to be imminent. Such a measure would be essential to prevent the complete devaluation of Bitcoin by ensuring only a minimal number of coins are affected.
Adoption hurdles are acknowledged as intrinsic to any PQC strategy, reflecting broader uncertainties about wallet providers' willingness to integrate such technologies. Despite skepticism about the universal adoption of PQC-enabled wallets, there's an acknowledgment that demand for such features exists, particularly among users interested in long-term storage solutions. This interest suggests that while some may disregard PQC capabilities for short-term applications, those planning for future security needs might prioritize these features when selecting wallets.
Moreover, the feasibility of excluding transactions from non-upgraded 'NUMS points' through a soft fork is discussed as a technical possibility, further underscoring the active exploration of solutions to secure Bitcoin against quantum threats. The discourse reflects a nuanced understanding of both the challenges and the imperative measures needed to safeguard Bitcoin's integrity in the face of advancing quantum computing technologies.
